title: Stagger, Liquid, Type, Gravity & Popover
description: Five live UI demos: CSS sibling-index stagger, Liquid Glass SVG refraction, per-letter variable font hover, gravity mouse trail, and zero-JS popover menus.

drops.title: sibling-index() Wave Stagger
drops.description: Pure CSS staggered animation using sibling-index() — each cell's delay is calc(sibling-index() * 0.04s). Zero JS timing.
drops.concept: CSS's new sibling-index() function lets you compute each element's ordinal position within its parent and use it in calc() expressions. Applied to animation-delay, this creates perfect stagger effects with zero JavaScript — the browser auto-assigns the index. Five wave modes (rise, spin, fade, scale, slide) show the versatility of this one-liner.
drops.source: Chrome for Developers · sibling-index() CSS feature (Chrome 133+)

interaction.mouse: Hover rows to highlight; choose wave mode buttons

drops.title: Liquid Glass Cards
drops.description: Apple iOS 26's Liquid Glass material on the web — SVG feTurbulence + feDisplacementMap + backdrop-filter: url() warps background pixels through glass.
drops.concept: Apple debuted Liquid Glass as the signature material of iOS 26/macOS 26, and web developers immediately began reproducing it. The technique chains an SVG filter (feTurbulence generating Perlin noise → feDisplacementMap warping pixels) applied via CSS backdrop-filter: url(#filter-id). Three intensity levels and three animation modes (static, flowing, pulse) demonstrate the effect's range.
drops.source: Apple iOS 26 / Rahul Chhodde · LogRocket Blog + kube.io

interaction.mouse: Hover cards to amplify refraction; click mode buttons

drops.title: Per-Letter Weight Hover
drops.description: Each character is an independent span. Mouse proximity drives font-weight transitions creating a tactile typographic wave across the word.
drops.concept: Per-letter variable font hover is the minimalist way to make typography feel alive. Each letter is wrapped in a span; CSS transitions animate font-weight from 200 to 900 (interpolated by the variable font engine), plus a scale and translateY lift. Mouse proximity drives data-level attributes, creating a ripple of weight that falls off with distance. Four modes (proximity, wave, center-out, random) show different interaction models.

interaction.mouse: Move cursor near letters to trigger weight animation

drops.title: Gravity Mouse Trail
drops.description: Elements spawn behind the cursor when movement threshold is hit, each falling under gravity with a damped bounce. DOM-only physics, no canvas.
drops.concept: A retro mouse trail reinvented with real physics. Rather than continuous particle spam, a distance accumulator spawns items only when you've moved enough — keeping performance clean. Each spawned element receives a random velocity and falls under configurable gravity, bouncing off a virtual floor with energy damping. Three shape modes (cards, circles, emoji) and a gravity-flip toggle show the versatility.
drops.source: Michael Garcia / Made With GSAP × Codrops · May 20 2026

interaction.mouse: Move cursor across the page to spawn trail items
interaction.click: Click control buttons to change shape or flip gravity

drops.title: Zero-JS Dropdown Menus
drops.description: Fully functional nested navigation menus built with HTML popover + CSS anchor-name/position-anchor. No JavaScript, no Popper.js, full keyboard support built in.
drops.concept: CSS Anchor Positioning hit Baseline 2026 status (Chrome 125+, Firefox 132+, Safari 18.2+) and when combined with the Popover API, it eliminates the need for any JavaScript dropdown library. The trigger uses popovertarget, the menu uses position-anchor to tether itself, @starting-style provides entrance animation, and the browser handles light-dismiss, Escape-key, and focus management automatically. Nested submenus work the same way.

interaction.mouse: Click nav buttons to open menus; click outside to dismiss
interaction.click: Click menu items; hover 'More ▸' for submenu
